chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Pett. No.D-1828 of 2025 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objections at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of CMA No.7230/2025 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 04.11.2025 Mr. Badaruddin Memon, Advocate along with petitioners Mr. Gulzar Ahmed Malano, Assistant Prosecutor General a/w SIP/IO Ghulam Akber Mr. Saeed Ahmed Wasan, Assistant Advocate General a/w respondent Kaloo Khan ************** Comments along with certain documents have been filed on behalf of respondents No.2& 3. Same are taken on record. A copy thereof has been supplied to the learned counsel for the petitioners. Investigating Officer/ SIP Ghulam Akber is present in Court. He submits that he has recorded the statement of petitioner No. 1 under Section 161, Cr.P.C. In her statement, petitioner No. 1 has not supported the contents of the FIR bearing Crime No.111/2025, registered at Police Station Daharki. She has stated that she was not abducted and that she entered into marriage with petitioner No.2 of her own free will and consent. In view of the said statement of petitioner No. 1, the investigation officer submits that the case is liable to be disposed of under “C” class. After such a statement, learned counsel for the petitioners seeks disposal of the instant petition. Accordingly, instant Constitution Petition, stands disposed of along with all pending application(s), with directions to the I.O to submit the report, as suggested by him, before the concerned Magistrate within seven days. In the meantime, official respondents are directed to provide legal protection to the petitioners in accordance with law.

Accordingly, instant Constitution Petition, stands disposed of along with all pending application(s), with directions to the I.O to submit the report, as suggested by him, before the concerned Magistrate within seven days. In the meantime, official respondents are directed to provide legal protection to the petitioners in accordance with law. J U D G E J U D G E M. Ali*
